---
applyTo: '**'
---

# i18n Instructions

## Bilingual content model

- Every user-visible content field that must appear in both languages uses `LocalizedText`:
  ```ts
  type LocalizedText = { ar: string; en: string };
  ```
- Every SEO field uses `LocalizedSeo`:
  ```ts
  type SeoFields = { title: string; description: string; keywords?: string[] };
  type LocalizedSeo = { ar: SeoFields; en: SeoFields };
  ```
- Never store content as separate Firestore documents per language
- Technical fields (`id`, `slug`, `status`, `category`, `createdAt`, etc.) are NOT localized

## Admin form layout

- Every localized field must expose **both** `ar` and `en` inputs simultaneously
- Group Arabic and English inputs visually (e.g. two-column layout or clearly labelled tabs)
- Clearly label `ar` fields with `العربية` and `en` fields with `English`
- Use `BilingualField` or `LocalizedTextField` shared form primitives

## Translation completeness validation

- Before a place (or any content item) can be published, both `ar` and `en` values for ALL required fields must be non-empty
- Required localized fields for places:
  - `title.ar`, `title.en`
  - `shortDescription.ar`, `shortDescription.en`
  - `description.ar`, `description.en`
  - `seo.ar.title`, `seo.ar.description`
  - `seo.en.title`, `seo.en.description`
- The publish action must be blocked (disabled button + validation error) if any required locale field is missing
- Use `checkTranslationCompleteness(formValues)` from `features/shared/validation/` to detect incomplete fields
- Surface per-locale errors (e.g. "Arabic title is required") in the form validation summary

## RTL readiness

- Arabic content inputs should have `dir="rtl"` and use the Arabic font (`--font-arabic: Cairo`)
- English content inputs should have `dir="ltr"` and use the Latin font (`--font-latin: Inter`)
- Admin form layout must work in both LTR (English admin UI) and RTL preview scenarios
- Use CSS logical properties (`margin-inline-start`, `padding-inline-end`) in shared components
- Preview areas for Arabic content should render with `dir="rtl"`

## Locale constants

- Locale values are always `'ar'` or `'en'` — never any other string
- Import `LOCALES`, `Locale`, `DEFAULT_LOCALE` from `@/lib/i18n/locales`
- Admin UI language defaults to English; Arabic is the content language, not necessarily the UI language

## Dictionary/strings

- Static admin UI strings live in `lib/i18n/dictionaries/en.ts` (admin UI is English)
- Do NOT create per-route dictionaries for admin UI strings
- Bilingual content data is stored in Firestore, not in dictionaries

## Helper functions

- `localise(text: LocalizedText, locale: Locale): string` — resolve one locale from `LocalizedText`
- `getDir(locale: Locale): 'rtl' | 'ltr'` — get text direction
- Import from `@/lib/i18n/helpers`
